name: Delta
product_tags: Only 2★ in Greece · Green Star · Ex-Maaemo chef
recognition: MICHELIN 2024 — two stars + Green Star, Greece's only two-star
what_to_order: Twelve-course Omnivore menu: Greek terroir driven vegetal and precise, the Saronic light on the glass.
notes: €100 deposit at booking; inside SNFCC so allow time for security and parking; Feskos left 2025, the kitchen is Papazacharias's alone; ~€240 pp for the 12-course Omnivore menu.
verdict: The only two-MICHELIN-star kitchen in Greece — and the 2026 Athinorama jury still ranked the solo Papazacharias kitchen joint-second in the country.
caveat: Not for a casual night out — a deposit-backed, full-evening gastronomic performance, not dinner.
iconic_orders: Grand tasting menu · Green Star vegetable courses · Greek-cellar wine pairing

name: Varoulko Seaside
recognition: Athinorama 2026 — Golden Hat, 16/20, level with the Riviera stars
what_to_order: Squid cut like tagliatelle: Lazarou's sweet, barely-set sleight, beside charcoal day-boat sea bass
best_time: Golden hour, caiques and Kastella lighting up
notes: STAR GONE: lost its Michelin star in the 2024 selection after 20+ years — still in the Guide's main selection; do not sell as 'Michelin-starred'. Still at Mikrolimano, no relocation. Book the waterfront tables.
verdict: Still the single most important seafood address in Piraeus — the 2026 Greek jury rates it level with the Riviera's starred rooms.
caveat: No longer a starred trophy booking, and not a cheap taverna night — come for the fish and the harbour, not the plaque.
iconic_orders: Catch of the day · Octopus, charred · Seafood tasting menu · Assyrtiko by the glass

name: Diporto
recognition: Athinorama 2024 — nominated for Greece's Intangible Cultural Heritage
what_to_order: Revithada: peppery chickpeas ladled straight from the pot, oil-slicked, bread-mopping hot
notes: CASH ONLY, no sign, no menu — you eat what kyr-Mitsos cooked. UNDER THREAT: 2024 reports said the building was sold with 1-2 years to vacate (owner-side denies); still open spring 2026 — go now.
verdict: There is no older or truer lunch in Athens: a 1913-lease wine cellar where the menu is whatever kyr-Mitsos decided at dawn.
caveat: Not for anyone needing a menu, a card machine, a clean floor plan or their own table — and maybe not here in two years.
iconic_orders: Fasolada · Patates giachni · Grilled sardines · Barrel retsina

name: Spondi
neighborhood: Pangrati · quiet street behind Varnava Square
recognition: MICHELIN 2024 — one star (formerly two), the grande dame of Athens fine dining
what_to_order: Signature tasting menu: classical French architecture over Aegean ingredients, three decades of polish, an Asian inflection.
notes: Dinner only, from 19:30; jacket-comfortable by Athens standards; kitchen now Bignon + head chef Demetzos (earlier chef Lantos gone); the tableside cheese trolley is the tell.
verdict: Still the most technically exacting French kitchen in Greece — a one-star room that plates like the two-star it used to be.
caveat: Not for seekers of 'modern Greek' — this is unapologetically French-school fine dining, jacket-comfortable.
iconic_orders: Breton scallop, white truffle, Lapsang Souchong · Cheese trolley · Grand dessert

name: Soil
neighborhood: Pangrati · Ferekydou, behind the Panathenaic Stadium
recognition: MICHELIN 2024 — one star + Green Star for sustainability
what_to_order: Seasonal tasting menu: ferments, wild herbs and heirloom vegetables treated with protein-level respect, quiet fire.
notes: Tasting-menu only, small room — book weeks ahead in season; on-site tasting listed around €110; the same team opened Amphibian at the Onassis Stegi in Jan 2026, this mothership continues.
verdict: The most convincing farm-to-table statement in Athens — a Green Star that actually tastes like one.
caveat: Not for diners who measure fine dining in caviar and lobster — the garden dictates the plate.
iconic_orders: Seasonal tasting menu · Vegetable-centred courses · Greek natural-leaning pairing

name: CTC Urban Gastronomy
recognition: MICHELIN 2024 — one star, held three years running
what_to_order: Eleven-course voyage: Greek memory dishes rebuilt with French technique and a straight-faced wit.
notes: Long ~11-course format, allow 3+ hours; @ctcresto on Instagram; Kerameikos edge — gritty street, serious room.
verdict: The clearest single expression of the new Athenian chef generation — Hytra-raised, French-drilled, Greek at heart.
caveat: Not for a quick dinner — the tasting is a three-hour, full-evening commitment.
iconic_orders: 11-course tasting · Bread service · Greek pairing flight

name: Makris Athens
product_tags: 1★ Michelin · Own farm · Vegan menu too
recognition: MICHELIN Dec 2024 — one star, the newest in central Athens
what_to_order: Genesis menu: own-farm produce and Greek heritage, modern technique, the Acropolis framed in the terrace glass.
notes: Newest Athens star (Dec 2024); three tasting menus incl. a full vegan Physis line; ask for the terrace — the glass floor shows the ancient Eleusis Inn.
verdict: Central Athens' newest Michelin star, and the only one that plates its own farm — book the terrace for the Acropolis as much as the food.
caveat: Not the address for classic-luxury, minimal-vegetable dining — the farm dictates the plate.
iconic_orders: Genesis tasting menu · Physis vegan menu · Utopia menu

name: GB Roof Garden
address: Vasileos Georgiou A' 1, Syntagma Square, 105 64 Athens
product_tags: Acropolis view · Wine Spectator cellar · MICHELIN Plate
recognition: MICHELIN — Plate, plus a long-standing Wine Spectator cellar award
what_to_order: Mediterranean plates and grand-hotel service, eight floors up, the lit Parthenon filling the whole terrace.
notes: Michelin Plate, NOT a star (the star belongs to sister Tudor Hall); the bar side works without a dinner booking; smart dress; renovated 2024.
verdict: The rooftop every Athens rooftop is measured against — 150 years of grand hotel below, the Acropolis full-frontal ahead.
caveat: You pay for the view twice — skip it if grand-hotel formality grates on you.
iconic_orders: Mediterranean tasting or à la carte fish · Acropolis-view cocktail · Wine Spectator cellar pick

name: Vezené
product_tags: Wood-fire · Dry-aged beef · Star Wine List
recognition: MICHELIN Guide listed + a Star Wine List cellar + a 2026 Greek Cuisine Award
what_to_order: Dry-aged steak off the wood fire, then 'Ari's Deal Closer' — swagger, not ceremony.
best_time: Late, when the fire hits cruising speed
notes: Wood-fired meat-and-fish bistro, not a tasting-menu house; Mon–Sat from 18:00, Sun lunch from 13:30; book — it's a chef-city fixture.
verdict: The chefs' choice for a night off — Athens' best wood-fire cooking, no tasting-menu ceremony attached.
caveat: Not budget, and not vegetarian-friendly at heart — this is a fire-and-meat room.
iconic_orders: Dry-aged steak from the fire · Whole fish, wood-grilled · Ari's Deal Closer · Serious-list wine

name: Sushimou
recognition: Athinorama Golden Hats 2026 — Audience Award (the diners' vote)
what_to_order: Omakase of Aegean fish cut edomae-style — twelve stools, one Tokyo-trained Greek, no menu.
notes: 12 stools only — book well ahead; omakase, chef decides, no substitutions; base sitting around €55 pp; three evening sittings.
verdict: The best sushi in Athens is Greek fish in Japanese hands — and the 2026 Golden Hats audience voted it so.
caveat: No menu, no groups, no lingering — omakase rules apply, and twelve seats fill fast.
iconic_orders: Omakase sitting · Local-fish nigiri · Sake pairing

name: Seychelles
recognition: THIS IS ATHENS 2026 — CTC's Alexandros Tsiotinis names it his no-frills Greek
what_to_order: Pappardelle with crab sauce: wide ribbons under sweet crab, the house's one non-negotiable order.
notes: Corner pavement tables are the point and largely first-come; kitchen opens ~13:30; relaunched 2023, not the pre-2013 Bahamas kitchen.
verdict: The starred chef of CTC names this his no-frills Greek benchmark, and the small-producer cooking holds up to the endorsement.
caveat: Not a dressed-up night out, and nothing 'exotic' despite the name — paper tablecloths, Greek register only.
iconic_orders: Pappardelle with crab sauce · Saganaki with crab · Beef liver with mustard & pickles · Tomato salad

name: Mavros Gatos
recognition: THIS IS ATHENS 2026 — Pharaoh's Manolis Papoutsakis calls lamb-and-retsina here 'an Athenian staple'
what_to_order: Paidakia: char-crusted thin lamb chops, eaten by hand with cold resinous retsina.
notes: Half-basement — look for the few steps down; keep the order classic (grill and starters), the paidakia are the whole argument.
verdict: Two of Athens' sharpest palates — Papoutsakis and LiFO — send you here for lamb chops, and the basement delivers on both counts.
caveat: Not for terrace romantics or the offal-shy — meat, charcoal smoke and old-taverna formica, nothing softened.
iconic_orders: Paidakia (lamb chops) · Homemade leek sausage · Grilled liver · Retsina

name: Psarokastella
neighborhood: Kastella, Piraeus · Skra lane above Mikrolimano
recognition: THIS IS ATHENS 2026 — Merceri's Maria Ntioudi keeps it as her secret eaterie
what_to_order: Day-boat fish, grilled: chosen that morning, dressed only in olive oil and lemon.
notes: No published hours or card policy — phone first; four-square-metre kitchen plus alley tables; a plain residential lane, no Mikrolimano view.
verdict: The Merceri chefs vouch that this family buys its fish daily — and every grilled plate off the Kastella veranda proves the habit.
caveat: Not a Mikrolimano quay show — no sea view, no theatre; you walk up a residential lane purely to eat.
iconic_orders: Day-boat fish, grilled · Taramosalata with bottarga · Garlic shrimp saganaki · Squid with capers

name: To Steki tou Ilia
neighborhood: Thissio · rail-side lane, Ano Petralona border
recognition: THIS IS ATHENS 2026 — Feyrouz's Andreas Kiltsiksis sends you for 'the tastiest lamb chops'
what_to_order: Paidakia: thin lamb chops char-edged off the coals, eaten with fingers under mulberry shade.
notes: Closed Mondays; two same-name houses on one lane (Thessalonikis 7 and Eptachalkou 5) — either works; order chops by the kilo.
verdict: A 1981 grill that has never needed a second idea — Feyrouz's chef and every Saturday queue agree no one grills a better lamb chop in Athens.
caveat: Vegetarians and slow-wine-dinner people have no business here — a grill with tables, and Monday it is shut.
iconic_orders: Paidakia · Lemonati sikotaria · Keftedakia · Horta

name: Feyrouz
what_to_order: Lahmajun, rolled hot: thin blistered dough, spiced beef, greens, sumac-and-lemon sharpness.
notes: Queue at peak lunch, mostly standing/stools; Antiochian, not Greek souvlaki; it ends when the dough does.
verdict: The best few-euro flatbread in central Athens — and a chef who skips it because 'it isn't Greek' has misread what Athens street food became.
caveat: Not the stop for souvlaki or a lingering table — Levantine counter food with a few stools, and it sells out.
iconic_orders: Lahmajun, rolled · Peinirli boat · Vegan lahmajun / greens · Pistachio sweets

name: Nolan
neighborhood: Syntagma · Voulis St, steps from the square
recognition: MICHELIN 2024 — Bib Gourmand, carried into the 2026 selection
what_to_order: Sea-bream tartare: Greek raw fish under Asian seasoning, one of the room's current signatures.
notes: Tiny; two sittings; 'Nolan 2.0' under Nourloglou since Dec 2024 — the Kontizas era is over.
verdict: Nolan's second act still earns its Bib: a Syntagma room where Greek produce speaks fluent Japanese, Vietnamese, Korean and Chinese.
caveat: Not a lingering-table restaurant — turnover across two sittings is part of the deal.
iconic_orders: Sea-bream tartare · Breaded beef cheeks with truffle · Rotating Greek-Asian plate

name: Marili
neighborhood: Kypseli · Agias Zonis pedestrian lane off Fokionos Negri
what_to_order: Khachapuri imeruli: molten fresh cow's-milk cheese in blistered dough, buttered straight from the clay tone.
notes: A bakery counter, not a table — takeaway or eat standing; tiny, cash-friendly; ring ahead, freshest press is 2023–25.
verdict: Athens' Georgian corner: a real tone-oven bakery where the khachapuri leaves the stone molten — the Caucasus answer to what here is special and not Greek.
caveat: Not a lingering table or a full dinner — a tiny bake-shop counter; ring ahead before a special trip up.
iconic_orders: Khachapuri (imeruli) · Filled dough pouches · Lavash & Georgian pites

name: Akra
neighborhood: Pangrati · Amynta St, near Proskopon Square
recognition: MICHELIN 2024 — Bib Gourmand, a new entry held into 2026
what_to_order: Daily fire-cooked market dish: the morning's best haul, seared over the continuously burning charcoal.
best_time: Late dinner, fire in full swing
notes: Entrance feature is the in-house bakery, not a butcher; loud, live-fire, half-portions offered; walk-ins wait at peak.
verdict: The Bib that defines new Pangrati — a daily-changing live-fire kitchen with a working bakery at its own front door.
caveat: Not for vegetarians or quiet-dinner seekers — it is loud, meat-forward and fire-led.
iconic_orders: Daily fire-cooked market dish · Wood-fired vegetables · Bakery pastry from the door

name: Pharaoh
product_tags: Bib Gourmand · Wood oven, no gas · 400+ natural wines
recognition: MICHELIN 2024 — Bib Gourmand, held into the 2026 selection
what_to_order: Wood-oven daily roast: an island or mountain recipe the blackboard picked that morning, slow-cooked in a kitchen with no gas.
notes: Menu rewritten almost nightly around market + wood oven; kitchen runs on no electricity or gas; restaurant, bar and wine shop in one.
verdict: Athens' best argument that tradition, a wood oven and natural wine make a modern restaurant — and we would cross town for the blackboard.
caveat: Menu control-freaks beware — you eat what the day decided, not what you planned.
iconic_orders: Wood-oven daily roast · Regional Greek classic of the day · Natural wine from the shop wall

name: Skalakia
recognition: THIS IS ATHENS 2026 — Pharaoh's Papoutsakis calls it his 'secret gem' for sweetbreads and liver
what_to_order: Grilled lamb sweetbreads: crisp outside, custard-soft within, lemon and a shot of tsipouro.
notes: Weekdays dinner-only from 16:00, Sunday lunch-only to 17:00 — time it right; the grilled sweetbreads are why you came.
verdict: A 1967 neighbourhood grill that outlived every Athens trend by changing nothing — Papoutsakis calls the sweetbreads and liver his secret gem, and he is right.
caveat: Not for the offal-shy or decor-hunters — unchanged-since-1967 consistency is the look, and Sunday dinner does not exist.
iconic_orders: Grilled lamb sweetbreads · Lemon-braised liver · Keftedakia · Fava

name: Ariston
neighborhood: Syntagma · Voulis 10, one block behind the square
recognition: GREEK STATE 2018 — declared a Monument of Modern Cultural Heritage
what_to_order: Tyropita kourou: half-moon of thick, buttery, crumbling crust around salty molten feta, eaten warm.
notes: Takeaway only, no seats since 1910; closed Sunday; sells through the morning bake — late afternoon is leftovers; pies travel well.
verdict: Over a century on, the tyropita kourou here is still the one to beat — order anything else first and you have wasted a warm one.
caveat: No tables, no coffee theatre — buy, walk, eat; and the trays empty with the morning.
iconic_orders: Tyropita kourou · Spanakopita · Melitzanopita · A dozen kourou for the boat

name: To Koulouri tou Psirri
what_to_order: A hot sesame koulouri at dawn: thin crackling ring, toasted sesame, steam in the cold.
notes: Working production bakery, no seats/coffee at the original counter; first hot trays ~05:00; adjacent café 'O Fournos tou Ilia' has seats.
verdict: Nearly every koulouri sold from a downtown street cart was born in this one Psyrri bakery — buy it here at 05:00 and the difference is the crunch.
caveat: Not for anyone wanting a seat, a coffee or a schedule — a flour-dusted production bakery with a till.
iconic_orders: Classic thin koulouri · Braided soft koulouri · A dozen rings for the crew

name: Olympion
neighborhood: Mets · Anapafseos, below the Panathenaic Stadium
product_tags: Mageireio · Window of pots · Under EUR 15
what_to_order: Kolokithakia gemista me avgolemono: courgettes stuffed and bound in egg-lemon, the tender-green house plate.
notes: Daytime cook-house, closed Sunday — go at lunch while the pots are full; takeaway tubs travel; hours unpublished, call to confirm.
verdict: The cheapest honest plate in the guide: read a Mets window of pots and eat the day's home cooking for less than a downtown cocktail.
caveat: No dinner service, no menu, no polish — a daytime window of pots on a residential lane; come at lunch or not at all.
iconic_orders: Kolokithakia gemista me avgolemono · Gemista · Moschari lemonato / kokkinisto · A tub of the day's mageirefta

name: Stani
recognition: THESSALONIKI FAIR 1953 — gold medal for its sheep's-milk yogurt
what_to_order: Sheep's-milk yogurt, honey and walnuts: cool, dense, faintly gamey, under mountain fir honey and crushed walnuts.
notes: Gritty Omonia block — go for the food, not the street; takeaway yogurt tubs travel; born in Piraeus 1931, refounded Omonia 1949.
verdict: The last true galaktopoleio in central Athens — sheep's-milk yogurt so dense the spoon stands upright, and no one in the centre does it better.
caveat: Not a café to linger in — no espresso ritual, no brunch plates, no laptops; you eat dairy the old way and leave.
iconic_orders: Sheep's-milk yogurt, honey & walnuts · Anthogalo with honey · Rizogalo · Fresh butter and kaimaki cream

name: Krinos
what_to_order: Loukoumades me meli: ring-shaped, crisp shell, molten crumb, honey syrup, heavy cinnamon, served scalding.
notes: Century-old room, tight at rush — takeaway window moves faster; the loukoumas is ring-shaped (Constantinople style), not the round norm.
verdict: A century on, no loukoumas in Athens has caught up with its honey-syruped, cinnamon-dusted ring.
caveat: Not for phyllo purists on a bougatsa pilgrimage, and not a lingering brunch room — marble, syrup and speed.
iconic_orders: Loukoumades me meli · Greek coffee · Bougatsa me krema

name: The Underdog
recognition: SCA — Michalis Dimitrakopoulos, 2016 World Coffee in Good Spirits Champion & 2019 World Barista finalist
what_to_order: Flat white on the house roast: silky, cocoa-dark, zero bitterness, beside warm chocolate babka.
notes: Flagship closes 19:00 — no late-night bar phase here; weekend brunch queues; micro-roastery on site; online booking, walk-ins wait.
verdict: Athens' most decorated coffee room also cooks the city's benchmark brunch — and the flat white will still outclass whatever lands on the plate.
caveat: Not for a quiet solo session at peak — weekend brunch is a scrum; but a weekday 08:00 espresso is exactly available.
iconic_orders: Flat white · Chocolate babka · Eggs Benedict · Coffee cocktail

name: Oinomageireio I Ipeiros
neighborhood: Varvakios Market · Filopoimenos side, off Athinas
product_tags: Since 1898 · Last in the market · Dawn patsas
what_to_order: White beef patsas: steam fogging the marble, spiked with your own skordostoumpi garlic-vinegar.
notes: Now keeps market hours ~07:00–18:00 — the dawn stop, NOT the 3 a.m. one; last patsatzidiko inside Varvakios; walk past hanging carcasses.
verdict: The last patsatzidiko inside the Varvakios meat market — the Athenian night's official final stop since 1898, now taken at dawn rather than at three.
caveat: Not for the 03:00 crowd anymore — it closes late afternoon; and not for anyone squeamish about hanging carcasses at breakfast.
iconic_orders: Patsas (white, beef) · Gida vrasti (boiled goat) · Magiritsa · Moschari kokkinisto

name: O Makis
what_to_order: Patsas chontrokommenos: coarse-cut tripe from the cauldron, lashed with garlic-vinegar and scarlet boukovo oil.
notes: 20–25 min taxi from the centre or Piraeus; 24/7, closed only Easter Sunday; the only patsatzidiko in Athens still using dedicated cauldrons.
verdict: Athens' hardest-working patsas cauldron: hundreds of portions a day, open every hour of the year except Easter Sunday.
caveat: Not a taverna evening — a fluorescent-lit working canteen deep in Peristeri; tripe-refusers get gyros, not poetry.
iconic_orders: Patsas chontrokommenos (coarse-cut) · Patsas psilokommenos (fine-cut) · Grills and gyros

name: Bougatsadiko I Thessaloniki stou Psyrri
neighborhood: Psyrri · Plateia Iroon corner, at Miaouli
what_to_order: Bougatsa me krema: warm custard, shattering phyllo, buried under cinnamon and icing sugar at dawn.
notes: 24h is weekend-only — Sun–Thu closes ~02:00; phyllo hand-opened on marble in the window; phone-check the late hours.
verdict: Psyrri's dawn bougatsa: hand-opened phyllo on Heroes' Square, and on weekends the pans do not stop for twenty-four hours.
caveat: Not a refined patisserie — square-side chairs, bar-crowd noise; and midweek the shutters come down around 02:00.
iconic_orders: Bougatsa me krema · Bougatsa me kima · Bougatsa me tyri

name: Elvis
what_to_order: Pork kalamaki, by the pair: salt, char and juice, with grilled pita quarters and a beer.
notes: No wraps — bare kalamakia only, order skewers by count; Kerameikos original runs to ~03:00, Fri/Sat ~05:00; standing-room tiny.
verdict: The Athenian kalamaki needs nothing but salt, charcoal and midnight — leave without ordering a second round of skewers and we got it wrong.
caveat: Not for wrap-seekers, daylight romantics or anyone needing a table — a butcher's-corner counter that peaks after midnight.
iconic_orders: Pork kalamaki, by the pair · Keftedes skewer · Grilled corn pita · Hand-cut fries

name: Ta Karamanlidika tou Fani
neighborhood: Psyrri edge · Sokratous & Evripidou, by Varvakios market
recognition: Athinorama 2018 & 2019 — Greek Cuisine tribute pick
what_to_order: Pastourma pie: fenugreek-crusted cured beef and molten cheese in crackling hot phyllo
reservation: Book — peak waits; rooftop tables go first
best_time: Lunch after the Varvakios market walk
notes: Closed Sunday; book or queue at peak. Deli counter vacuum-packs pastourma/soutzouki that survive a week in a galley fridge. A second branch exists at Ermou 119 — this is the original Sokratous/Evripidou flagship.
verdict: The pastourma pie and the deli counter alone justify crossing town; everything past the cured-meat canon is merely good.
caveat: Not for vegetarians or the cumin-averse, and dark on Sundays.
iconic_orders: Karamanli pastourma platter · Pastourma with eggs · Grilled soutzouki · Vacuum-packed pastourma

name: Miran
what_to_order: Aged veal pastourma: paper-thin mahogany slices under a faintly bitter mustard-yellow tsimeni crust
price_range: Deli — pay by weight; counter tasting plates
reservation: No booking — a shop with a tasting corner
best_time: Late morning, spice trade in full cry
notes: Closed Sunday; shop hours only, no evening service — call ahead. It's a deli with a tasting corner, not a restaurant. Vacuum-packed pastourma is the best edible souvenir of 1922 Athens for a galley. The 'largest EU producer' line is the company's own uncorroborated claim.
verdict: No shop in Athens sells better pastourma, and the standing counter-tasting outclasses most sit-down meze in the quarter.
caveat: Not a restaurant — no full meals, no evening hours, strictly carnivore territory.
iconic_orders: Armenian soutzouk · Counter tasting board · Rarity pastourmas

name: To Aigyptiakon
recognition: LiFO 2021 — profiled as one of Greece's best kebabs
what_to_order: Kebab yogurtlou: charcoal-grilled hand-minced kebab on pita under warm tomato, sumac and yogurt
best_time: Right at the 17:00 opening, before the queue
notes: Evenings only and closed Sunday; ~25 min from central Athens — pair with a Kokkinia refugee-streets walk. Right at the 17:00 opening beats the queue. Price is an estimate (souvlaki-house tier).
verdict: Greece's first kebab house, grilling since 1924, still makes one of its best — worth the ride from the port on a Saturday evening.
caveat: Not central and not scenic — a working-class Nikaia avenue, dinner-only, zero frills.
iconic_orders: Adana kebab in pita · Falafel · Lahmajun · Charcoal tiropita

name: Peran, to Kafe Aman
neighborhood: Nea Chalkidona · Dekeleias, northern refugee belt
product_tags: Politiki kouzina · Live cafe aman · House-baked
recognition: Athinorama 2026 — the guide's only politiki-kouzina table
what_to_order: Yogurt kebab with cumin: juicy, gently spicy, bleeding into the house-baked bread
best_time: Fri/Sat night for sets; Sun afternoon for lunch
notes: Open Wed-Sat evenings plus Sunday afternoon only. Book on live-music nights — the sets fill the stone room. Appetisers chosen off a passing tray; bread and sweets baked in-house.
verdict: The last politiki-kouzina table standing in Athinorama's guide, and the live cafe-aman nights alone justify crossing the refugee belt.
caveat: Not a quick stop — music nights run long and loud, and it's closed Monday to Wednesday lunch.
iconic_orders: Meze tray selection · Hunkar begendi · House Politiki sweets

name: Kostas (Agia Irini)
neighborhood: Monastiraki · Plateia Agias Eirinis, by the church
what_to_order: Souvlaki me ap'ola, hot sauce: charred pork, chopped tomato, the fiery house 'farmako' red.
notes: Sells out then shutters — arrive before 15:00; wrap was EUR 3 (Feb 2025); NOT the unrelated 'since 1950' Kostas on Filellinon; carry cash.
verdict: The three-euro wrap from this postwar hole-in-the-wall still beats every chef-built souvlaki in Athens — arrive after 15:00 and the meat is likely gone.
caveat: Not for sit-down comfort or late lunches — no tables, and no mercy once the day's meat is finished.
iconic_orders: Souvlaki me ap'ola, hot sauce · Kalamaki sketo · Bifteki wrap, medium sauce

name: Hoocut
neighborhood: Monastiraki · Plateia Agias Eirinis 9, at Aiolou
recognition: THIS IS ATHENS 2026 — CTC's Alexandros Tsiotinis: 'as genuine as it gets, as clean as it should be today'
what_to_order: Pork-belly pita: crackling edge, molten fat and bright raw onion on bread baked metres away.
notes: NOT Michelin-listed despite blog claims; pita kneaded and baked on-site, meat from their own Metameat plant; peak-hour queues.
verdict: Five acclaimed chefs run Athens' most disciplined souvlaki, and a Michelin-starred peer calls it 'as genuine as it gets' — the pita out of the oven proves both.
caveat: Costs more than a corner souvlatzidiko and there's no table service — nostalgics for the three-euro paper-wrapped classic should walk thirty metres to Kostas.
iconic_orders: Pork belly pita · Lamb pita · Beef short-rib pita · Hand-cut fries

name: O Thanasis
neighborhood: Monastiraki · Mitropoleos, at the square's mouth
what_to_order: Kebab merida on pita: charcoal-charred lamb-beef mince with raw onion, tomato and a paprika dust.
notes: The legendary charcoal KEBAB of Monastiraki, not skewer souvlaki — order accordingly; heavy tourist flow, daily queues.
verdict: Since 1964, 'kebab' in Athens has meant this exact spiced, charcoal-grilled cylinder at Mitropoleos 69 — still the centre's most dependable plate after midnight.
caveat: Not for anyone chasing quiet or the bare pork kalamaki — a loud, touristic, table-service kebab institution, and that is the point.
iconic_orders: Kebab merida on pita · Kebab me yiaourti · Roasted peppers + tzatziki · House red in a small jug

name: Klimataria
neighborhood: Plateia Theatrou · on the square, under the vine
recognition: Athens Voice 2024 — 'the emblematic taverna of Plateia Theatrou'
what_to_order: Lamb kotsi melomeno: honey-glazed shank collapsing off the bone into its own juices
best_time: Fri/Sat ~22:30; Sunday lunch for the pots
notes: Live music roughly Wed-Sat (venue site) though Athinorama lists Fri-Sun — confirm the night. Music nights can carry a cover/bread charge — ask. Square is scruffy after dark; taxi to the door.
verdict: The last taverna in central Athens where barrel wine, hand-cooked mageirefta and live rebetes still share one 1927 room several nights a week.
caveat: Not a quiet dinner on music nights — after 22:00 conversation surrenders to the band; on off-nights you find only the kitchen.
iconic_orders: Soutzoukakia · Giouvetsi · Handmade pies and bread · Barrel wine

name: Taverna tou Oikonomou
neighborhood: Ano Petralona · Troon & Kydantidon, opposite Zefyros cinema
recognition: FNL Guide — 'a journey into the memory of Greek tradition'
what_to_order: Rooster with hilopites: wine-dark bird over egg noodles drinking the slow-cooked sauce
reservation: Book weekends — pavement tables go first
best_time: Summer evening outside, Zefyros flickering opposite
notes: MAGEIREFTA HOUSE — the day's pots run out; go early and hungry. 2023 handover kept the same cook (Garoufallia) and walls. Weekend pavement tables opposite Zefyros cinema go first.
verdict: The 1930 butcher's-taverna where Athens still measures what home-stewed rooster and hilopites should taste like.
caveat: Not for grill-seekers or late arrivals — the glory is in the pots, and the pots empty.
iconic_orders: Rabbit stifado · Soutzoukakia · Lamb in the oven · Summer okra and beans

name: Margaro
neighborhood: Piraeus, Hatzikyriakio · Naval Cadets' School gate
recognition: Athinorama 2025 re-review — 'the old neighbourhood fish taverna still lives'
what_to_order: Fried barbounia: flour-whispered red mullet, scalding hot, sweet flesh under crackling skin
notes: No reservations + recited 4-5-item menu (no printed carte); Sunday waits are real, go weekday ~13:00. Closed Tuesday. Card acceptance unverified — carry cash.
verdict: Eighty years on, a recited menu of five fried things still beats every printed seafood carte in Piraeus.
caveat: Not for menu-browsers, vegetarians, bookers, or anyone who needs dessert and a sea view.
iconic_orders: Fried garides (shrimp) · Karavides (langoustines) · Tomato salad · House white by the kilo

name: Dourabeis
neighborhood: Piraeus · Akti Dilaveri, foot of Kastella
recognition: FNL Guide — 'reference point of classic Athenian fish-eating'
what_to_order: Charcoal-grilled wild fish with ladolemono: salt-sweetness and smoke, dressed old-school, chosen by the kilo
price_range: Fish by the kilo; budget EUR 60+ pp
best_time: Winter Sunday lunch, the classicists' hour
notes: Serious-restaurant prices — stopped being a taverna decades ago; budget EUR 60+ pp (unverified, Athinorama lists EUR 45-55). Book. Fish by the kilo, landed from Kythira/Evia/Koroni/Cyclades/Crete. No published hours — call.
verdict: Ninety-plus years of charcoal and ladolemono make it the control experiment for every Athens fish kitchen — the plainest possible plate, done better.
caveat: Not cheap and not a taverna lark — white-linen fish-house gravity, priced to match.
iconic_orders: Dourabeis salad · Linguine with oysters · Shellfish selection

name: Zefyros
neighborhood: Piraeus · Mikrolimano quay, deck over the water
product_tags: Since 1931 · Mikrolimano's oldest · Deck over water
recognition: Athinorama 2025 — 'the oldest fish house of Mikrolimano, 94 years and counting'
what_to_order: Whole day-boat fish, grilled plain: eaten on planks built out over the harbour water
best_time: Dusk on the deck, Kastella lighting up
notes: Priced for the view; fish by the kilo (per-head band unverified). Book the waterside deck. Est. 1931 — Mikrolimano's oldest fish house; classic cooking, no fusion.
verdict: The one Mikrolimano quay terrace where the history is real: the harbour's oldest fish house, still feeding the classicists.
caveat: Skip it if you want modern cooking or a bargain — this is classic, at view prices.
iconic_orders: Oysters and raw shellfish · Seasonal seafood mezedes · Greek-vineyard whites

name: To Kapileio tou Zachou
recognition: Athinorama Jan 2026 — 'fish like the old days, unchanged'
what_to_order: Fried koutsomoura: small red mullet straight from the pan, crisp, sweet, barrel wine beside
notes: CARD ACCEPTANCE UNVERIFIED — carry cash. Ten minutes inland from the cruise terminal (no sea view). Phone ahead weekends. Athinorama's Jan 2026 verdict: 'unchanged', in the best sense.
verdict: Kaminia's proof that fried small fish, barrel wine and an Olympiacos shrine can outclass the Mikrolimano quay.
caveat: Not for sea views, card-only wallets, or anyone who needs polish — inland, local, unvarnished.
iconic_orders: Shrimp saganaki · Mussel pilaf · Courgette-and-tomato fritters · Barrel wine
